commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

This PR simplifies recently modified code in the Inspector V2

if/else statement for better readability

- ✅ No unintended side effects

perf: parallelize programs + staff DB queries in provider dashboard mount

Both ProgramCatalog.list_programs_for_provider/1 and Provider.list_staff_members/1
are independent DB queries fired sequentially in the dashboard mount. Running them
concurrently with Task.async/await saves ~1 DB round-trip worth of wall time
(typically 5–15ms) per provider dashboard page load.

Implement Entity Source Generator

Implement the 5 core files of the entity source generator:

- EntityGenerator.cs: Main entry using ForAttributeWithMetadataName to
  discover [Entity] classes, parse columns/indexes/DetailOf attributes,
  map CLR types to PostgreSQL types, and invoke all 4 emitters

- DalEmitter.cs: Generates CREATE TABLE DDL with proper column types,
  NOT NULL constraints, PRIMARY KEY, PARTITION BY LIST for tenant tables,
  audit table DDL, and CREATE INDEX statements

- CrudEmitter.cs: Generates INSERT/SELECT/DELETE SQL constants,
  BuildInsertParams helper with proper NpgsqlParameter/NpgsqlDbType
  mapping, and ReadEntity method with correct reader methods

- AuditCrudEmitter.cs: Generates audit table name constant and
  DiffAuditSnapshots method using System.Text.Json.JsonDocument for
  NativeAOT-safe snapshot comparison

- DescEmitter.cs: Generates table name/tenant constants, Fields nested
  class with column name constants, DictFieldMetas dictionary with
  YTFieldMeta entries, and GetFieldMeta lookup method

Also adds <Compile Remove="Generated/**" /> to the sample project to
prevent double-compilation of emitted files.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add encoding parameter to Frame.ReadCsv path/stream overloads (Closes #346) (#617)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds an optional `encoding` parameter to `Frame.ReadCsv` overloads that
accept a file path or `Stream`, so users can read CSV files with
non-UTF-8 encodings without having to construct a `StreamReader`
manually.

Closes #346.

## Root Cause

The path- and stream-based `ReadCsv` overloads always constructed `new
StreamReader(path)` / `new StreamReader(stream)` with no encoding
argument, defaulting to UTF-8. The `TextReader` overload was already
encoding-agnostic, but it required users to build their own
`StreamReader` — a workaround that was not obvious.

## Changes

- `src/Deedle/FrameExtensions.fs`
- F# `ReadCsv(path, ..., ?encoding)` — passes encoding to `StreamReader`
when provided
  - F# `ReadCsv<'R>(path, indexCol, ..., ?encoding)` — same
  - F# `ReadCsv(stream, ..., ?encoding)` — same
- C# `ReadCsv(location, ..., encoding)` — same (optional `[(Optional)]`
parameter, `null` = default)
  - C# `ReadCsv(stream, ..., encoding)` — same
  - Added `open System.Text` for convenient `Encoding` usage
- `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs`
- Added two regression tests for Latin-1 encoding via path and stream
overloads

## Usage

**F#:**
```fsharp
open System.Text

// Read a Latin-1 encoded CSV
let df = Frame.ReadCsv("file.csv", encoding = Encoding.GetEncoding("iso-8859-1"))

// Read a Windows-1252 CSV from a stream
use stream = File.OpenRead("data.csv")
let df2 = Frame.ReadCsv(stream, encoding = Encoding.GetEncoding(1252))
```

**C#:**
```csharp
using System.Text;

var df = Frame.ReadCsv("file.csv", encoding: Encoding.GetEncoding("iso-8859-1"));
```

## Test Status

✅ All 496 tests pass (494 existing + 2 new encoding tests).

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Stats.describe for Frame (pandas-style per-column summary statistics) (#612)

🤖 *Repo Assist — automated AI improvement.*

Closes #398

## Summary

Implements `Stats.describe` for `Frame<'R,'C>` (and corresponding
`FrameStatsExtensions.Describe` for C#), a long-requested (#398)
pandas-style summary statistics method.

### What it does

Calling `Stats.describe frame` produces a `Frame(string, 'C)` with one
column per numeric column of the input frame, and these row keys:

| Key | Meaning |
|-----|---------|
| `unique` | number of unique values |
| `mean` | arithmetic mean |
| `std` | sample standard deviation |
| `min` | minimum |
| `0.25` | 25th percentile |
| `0.5` | median |
| `0.75` | 75th percentile |
| `max` | maximum |

Only columns convertible to `float` are included (via
`frame.GetColumns(float)()`). Non-numeric columns are silently dropped,
matching the behaviour of pandas.

### Usage (F#)

```fsharp
let df = Frame.ofColumns [ "Open", openSeries; "Close", closeSeries ]
let summary = Stats.describe df
// summary is a Frame(string, string)
// summary.["Open", "mean"]  -> mean of Open column
// summary.["Close", "min"]  -> min of Close column
```

### Usage (C#)

```csharp
var summary = df.Describe();
// summary["Open", "mean"]  -> mean of Open column
```

## Root cause / approach

`Stats.describe` already exists for `Series<'K,'V>` and returns a
`Series(string, float)`. The frame variant simply maps this function
over all numeric columns via `frame.GetColumns(float)() |> Series.map
(fun _ s -> Stats.describe s)` and assembles the result into a frame
using `FrameUtils.fromColumns`.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/Deedle/Stats.fs` | Added `Stats.describe (frame:Frame<'R,'C>)` |
| `src/Deedle/FrameStatsExtensions.fs` | Added `Describe()` C# extension
method |
| `tests/Deedle.Tests/Stats.fs` | Added `describe works for frame` test
|

## Test Status

✅ All 495 existing tests pass.  
✅ New test `describe works for frame` passes.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Series.pctChange and Frame.pctChange for percentage change calculations (#610)

🤖 *Repo Assist — automated AI improvement.*

## Summary

Adds `Series.pctChange` and `Frame.pctChange` functions that compute the
relative (percentage) change between each value and the value at a given
offset:

```
result[k] = (series[k] - series[k - offset]) / series[k - offset]
```

This is the standard way to compute financial returns (e.g. daily stock
returns from a price series) and is the F# equivalent of pandas'
`pct_change`.

## Motivation

The homepage example (see #517) attempts to calculate daily returns
using `Frame.diff 1 / df`, which is incorrect because `diff` gives the
**absolute** change and dividing by `df` gives something subtly
different from `(df - shift(df,1)) / shift(df,1)`. A dedicated
`pctChange` function removes this confusion and simplifies the idiomatic
usage.

**Before** (current — from homepage docs):
```fsharp
let past = df |> Frame.diff 1
let rets = past / df * 100.0   // Wrong: divides diff by current, not by previous
```

**After**:
```fsharp
let rets = df |> Frame.pctChange 1   // Correct: (curr - prev) / prev
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/Deedle/SeriesModule.fs` | Add `Series.pctChange` inline function
(SRTP, like `Series.diff`) |
| `src/Deedle/FrameModule.fs` | Add `Frame.pctChange` function (float
columns only, like `Frame.diff`); update module doc example |
| `src/Deedle/SeriesExtensions.fs` | Add `PctChange` C# extension
methods for `float`, `float32`, `decimal` |
| `src/Deedle/FrameExtensions.fs` | Add `PctChange` C# extension method
|
| `tests/Deedle.Tests/Series.fs` | 3 new tests: basic, empty/short
series, missing value propagation |
| `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs` | 2 new tests: basic computation, empty
frame |

## Test Status

✅ All 498 existing tests pass  
✅ All 5 new `pctChange` tests pass  
Build: no new warnings introduced by these changes

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Frame.renameCol and Frame.renameColsUsing module functions (Closes #62) (#603)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Adds two non-mutating module-level functions to address the decade-old
feature request in #62.

## Root Cause / Motivation

`df.RenameColumn(oldKey, newKey)` and `df.RenameColumns(mapping)` are
only available as mutating members. There were no functional
(pipe-friendly) module equivalents. This is an inconsistency with other
`Frame` module functions like `addCol`, `dropCol`, `replaceCol`, and
`indexColsWith`.

## Changes

Two new functions added to `FrameModule.fs`:

```fsharp
// Rename a single column by key
let renamed = df |> Frame.renameCol "OldName" "NewName"

// Rename all columns via a mapping function
let lower = df |> Frame.renameColsUsing (fun k -> k.ToLowerInvariant())
```

Both follow the same pure-construction pattern as `indexColsWith` —
creating a new `Frame` directly without Clone+mutate — so no
intermediate allocation is needed.

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `Frame.renameCol oldKey newKey` | Renames one column; if `oldKey` is
not found, the frame is returned unchanged |
| `Frame.renameColsUsing mapping` | Renames all columns by applying a
mapping function; changes the column key type |

Note: `Frame.indexColsWith` already covers the case of replacing *all*
column keys with a new sequence. The two new functions complement it by
supporting single-column rename and function-based renaming.

## Test Status

✅ All 468 tests pass (`dotnet test
tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj -c Release`).

4 new tests cover:
- single-column rename
- rename with an unknown key (no-op)
- `renameColsUsing` transforms all keys
- immutability (original frame is not mutated)

## Trade-offs

- `renameColsUsing` changes the column key type (e.g., `string -> int`),
consistent with `indexColsWith`. This is intentional and matches the
member `RenameColumns(mapping)`.
- `renameCol` returns the frame unchanged when `oldKey` is not found,
matching the silent no-op behaviour of the underlying member.

Closes #62

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Frame.meltBy for pandas-style melt with identity columns (#598)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds `Frame.meltBy`, a new frame-transformation function that keeps
specified **identity columns** as-is and melts the remaining columns
into `Column`/`Value` pairs. This is analogous to pandas
`DataFrame.melt(id_vars=...)` and R's `data.table::melt`.

Closes #554.

## Usage

```fsharp
let df =
    frame [ "ticker" =?> series [ 0 => "AAPL"; 1 => "MSFT" ]
            "price"  =?> series [ 0 => 189.5; 1 => 415.2 ]
            "volume" =?> series [ 0 => 1_200_000.0; 1 => 800_000.0 ] ]

let melted = df |> Frame.meltBy ["ticker"]
// Result:
// ticker | Column | Value
// -------+--------+----------
// AAPL   | price  | 189.5
// AAPL   | volume | 1200000.0
// MSFT   | price  | 415.2
// MSFT   | volume |  800000.0
```

## Implementation

- **`Frame.meltBy (idCols: seq<'C>) (frame: Frame<'R, 'C>)`** in
`FrameModule.fs`
- Accepts any `seq<'C>` of identity column keys (uses `HashSet` for O(1)
lookup)
- Output frame: integer row index, string column keys (`[id cols...] +
"Column" + "Value"`)
- Preserves the original element type of each identity column via
`createTypedVector`
- Infers the common supertype for the `Value` column (same strategy as
`Frame.melt`)

## Tests

4 new tests in `Frame.fs`:
- Row count is correct (`rows × value_cols`)
- Output column names include id columns + `Column` + `Value`
- Id column values are repeated correctly
- Id column element types are preserved

## Test Status

✅ Build succeeded (0 errors, 4 warnings — all pre-existing)
✅ All 468 tests pass (including 4 new `meltBy` tests)

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Frame.distinctRowsBy to remove duplicate rows by column values (#596)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds `Frame.distinctRowsBy`, a new frame-transformation function that
retains only the **first row** (by index order) for each unique
combination of values in the specified columns.

This is analogous to SQL `SELECT DISTINCT col1, col2 FROM table` — a
pattern that comes up regularly (see #558).

## Example

```fsharp
// Given a frame with duplicate rows:
//   A  B   C
// 0 x  1.0 10
// 1 y  2.0 20
// 2 x  1.0 30   ← duplicate of row 0 on (A, B)
// 3 y  2.0 40   ← duplicate of row 1 on (A, B)

df |> Frame.distinctRowsBy ["A"; "B"]
//   A  B   C
// 0 x  1.0 10
// 1 y  2.0 20
```

C#:
```csharp
df.DistinctRowsBy("A", "B")
```

## Implementation

`distinctRowsBy` is built on top of the existing `filterRows` primitive.
For each row, it computes a key as an `obj list` of the requested column
values. F# lists have structural equality and `GetHashCode()` so
`HashSet(obj list)` correctly deduplicates any combination of standard
.NET value types (strings, ints, floats, DateTime, etc.).

A C#-friendly `[(Extension)] DistinctRowsBy(frame, [(ParamArray)]
columns)` overload is added to `FrameExtensions`.

## Test Status

- `src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj` — **builds without errors** ✅
- `tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj` — **compiles without errors**
✅
- Unit tests cannot be executed locally (requires .NET 5, environment
has .NET 8+); three new tests cover partial deduplication, no-op, and
full deduplication. Please verify via CI.

## Trade-offs

- Uses `obj list` key rather than a typed tuple — keeps the
implementation simple and avoids arity limits, at the cost of boxing
value types per row per call. For large frames with many unique columns,
a custom `IEqualityComparer` could improve performance, but this is
sufficient for typical use.
- Missing values are treated as `null` in the key (two rows with missing
values in the same columns are considered equal).

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Frame.toJson / saveJson for JSON serialization (#609)

🤖 *Repo Assist — automated AI implementation in response to maintainer
request.*

Closes #556

## Summary

Implements JSON serialization for Deedle data frames, mirroring pandas'
`to_json` API. No new dependencies are introduced — JSON is written
using a simple `TextWriter`-based approach.

## New API

### F# module functions
```fsharp
// Returns a JSON string
df |> Frame.toJson "columns"   // column-major (default)
df |> Frame.toJson "index"     // row-major
df |> Frame.toJson "records"   // array of row objects

// Writes to a file
df |> Frame.saveJson "/tmp/out.json"
```

### F# extension methods
```fsharp
df.ToJson()                       // defaults to "columns"
df.ToJson(orient = "records")
df.SaveJson("out.json")
df.SaveJson(writer)
```

### C# static methods
````csharp
Frame.ToJson(df)                  // "columns" orient
Frame.ToJson(df, "records")
Frame.SaveJson(df, "out.json")
Frame.SaveJson(df, writer)
```

## JSON orient modes

| orient | shape | example |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `"columns"` | column-major object | `{"col":{"row":v,...},...}` |
| `"index"` | row-major object | `{"row":{"col":v,...},...}` |
| `"records"` | array of row objects | `[{"col":v,...},...]` |

## Design notes

- **No new dependencies** — plain `TextWriter` with manual JSON
escaping.
- `float`/`float32` NaN and ±Infinity → `null` (matching pandas default
behaviour).
- `DateTime`/`DateTimeOffset` serialised as ISO 8601 round-trip strings
(`"o"` format).
- All string keys and values are properly JSON-escaped (quotes,
backslash, control chars).
- `OptionalValue(T)` column types are unwrapped, consistent with
`SaveCsv`.
- Multi-level row keys are flattened to a single string with `" - "` as
separator.

## Test status

```
All tests passed: 494 total (9 new JSON tests + 485 pre-existing)
````

Built and tested on Linux with `dotnet test
tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj -c Release`.

* Fix fillForm() to update WageWizard.LeagueDetails after restoring league from params URL

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include nation in CHPP link; sync per-team league

- Add LeagueID to each team's array in getTeamDetails so multi-team
  users carry the correct league for each team
- In updateCHPPPlayerFields, set WageWizard_League select value and
  WageWizard.LeagueDetails from the active team's LeagueID so that
  formSerialize() captures the correct nation in the generated link,
  even though the league select is hidden in CHPP mode

* test

* Populate league select before setting CHPP nation

createCountryDropbox() was only called in non-CHPP mode, leaving
the WageWizard_League select with no <option> elements in CHPP
mode. Setting .value on an empty select is a no-op, so the team's
country ID was never actually stored in the field and was missing
from the serialized link. Call createCountryDropbox() in the
CHPP OK path before setupCHPPPlayerFields so the select has
options when updateCHPPPlayerFields sets its value.

* Remove named URL params recognition

Drop fillFormFromPlayerParams and PLAYER_URL_PARAMS. The named
?goalkeeping=X&age=Y&nationality=Z parameter scheme is no longer
needed; the standard ?params= serialized format is the only
supported way to pre-fill the form from a URL.

---------

commit.message:
feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement audit event query service (T074)

Add AuditQueryService with getEntityTimeline() and searchAuditEvents()
for querying the append-only audit log with flexible, combinable filters
and offset-based pagination.

- New AuditEventQueryPort (read-side, CQRS-lite separation from write port)
- AuditEventFilters: entityType, entityId, eventType, actorType, actorId, startTime, endTime
- PaginatedAuditResult with total count and page metadata
- Input validation (non-empty entity params, time range consistency)
- Pagination normalization (clamping, defaults)
- Infrastructure: findByEntityPaginated() and searchFiltered() with Drizzle dynamic queries
- Infrastructure: createAuditEventQueryPortAdapter() with shared mapRowToRecord()
- 26 unit tests covering timelines, filters, pagination, validation, edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
